# Video Compressor

> A free, 100% client-side video compression tool that runs entirely in the browser. No file uploads, no servers, no privacy concerns.

## What it does

Video Compressor lets users reduce video file sizes directly in their browser using either WebCodecs (GPU-accelerated, Chrome/Edge) or FFmpeg.wasm (universal fallback for Firefox/Safari). The compressed file is downloaded locally — nothing is ever sent to a server.

## Key features

- **100% private**: Files never leave the user's browser
- **GPU acceleration**: Uses the WebCodecs API with hardware encoding on supported browsers
- **Universal fallback**: FFmpeg.wasm handles browsers without WebCodecs support
- **Quality control**: Adjustable CRF value (18–40) for fine-grained quality/size tradeoff
- **Resolution selection**: Original, 1080p, 720p, or 480p
- **Audio removal**: Optional stripping of the audio track
- **Before/after comparison**: Displays original and compressed file sizes with reduction percentage

## Technology

- Frontend: Vite + Vanilla TypeScript (no framework)
- Compression Engine A: WebCodecs API + mp4-muxer (GPU-accelerated, Chrome/Edge 94+)
- Compression Engine B: @ffmpeg/ffmpeg + @ffmpeg/core-mt (multithreaded WASM, universal)
- No backend, no database, no analytics

## Supported formats

Input: MP4, MOV, AVI, MKV, and other common video formats (up to 2GB)
Output: MP4 (H.264)

## Source code

https://github.com/Guksu/video-encoder

## Live URL

https://video-encoder-henna.vercel.app/
